moat.description: The only company in the world capable of producing EUV machines required for <7nm chips.
moat.analysisSummary: ASML is the **Sole Provider** of the world's most complex machines:

analysisPoints.title: EUV Monopoly
analysisPoints.text: Extreme Ultraviolet (EUV) lithography is required for every advanced chip from Apple, Nvidia, and Intel. ASML is the only company that can build them.

analysisPoints.title: Technological Barrier
analysisPoints.text: Developing EUV took 20+ years and billions in funding. A competitor would need decades to catch up.

analysisPoints.title: Service Ecosystem
analysisPoints.text: Once a machine is installed (costing $200M+), ASML generates recurring service revenue for 20+ years.

growth.description: Moore's Law continues to drive demand for High-NA EUV and increased wafer capacity.

valuation.description: Near base case (~$1,451 vs $1,500 base). ASML has exceeded its prior bull target. China restriction risk and semiconductor cycle timing are the key headwinds.

bear.description: Full DUV/EUV export ban to China, coupled with a broad semiconductor capex pause as demand normalises.
points: Banning of older DUV machine exports to China
Slowdown in capacity expansion from major foundries (TSMC/Intel/Samsung)
Cyclical downturn in consumer electronics demand

base.description: High-NA EUV rollout continues on schedule, 2nm/1.4nm capacity build sustains demand, China revenue stabilises at a lower but steady floor.
points: Successful scaling of High-NA EUV production
China revenue stabilized at lower but steady levels

bull.description: Sovereign foundry programmes accelerate globally (US, Europe, Japan, Middle East), High-NA becomes the dominant advanced-node architecture.
points: Nations building domestic chip capacity significantly increases demand
AI-driven demand for HBM and advanced logic exceeds all forecasts
Substantial margin expansion from 'high-end' product mix shift

learnedInterfaces.note: Not applicable — ASML sells precision lithography hardware to chip fabricators, not UI-based software.

businessLogic.note: Not applicable to extreme ultraviolet lithography physics and optics manufacturing.

publicDataAccess.note: Not applicable to this hardware monopoly business model.

talentScarcity.note: EUV optics engineers, plasma physicists, and precision mechatronic specialists are among the scarcest people on Earth — AI cannot replicate them.

bundling.note: Full EUV system bundle: laser light source + optics + scanner + metrology + software + service = inseparable, multi-year integration.

proprietaryData.note: 30+ years of machine performance data from every chip fab run generates yield-optimization intelligence no competitor can replicate.

regulatoryLockIn.note: Dutch export-controlled technology requires government approval per sale; TSMC/Samsung/Intel dependency = state-protected monopoly.

networkEffects.note: Deep co-development partnerships with TSMC, Samsung, and Intel create mutual IP dependency and long-term co-investment.

transactionEmbedding.note: ASML EUV machines run 24/7 inside fabs generating the chips the world runs on — there is no alternative mid-production-cycle.

systemOfRecord.note: Sole supplier of EUV lithography — ASML IS the system of record for cutting-edge semiconductor manufacturing globally.

tenMoats.verdict: ASML is the most AI-resilient company in the portfolio. Physical hardware monopolies, regulatory protection, and manufacturing expertise rooted in physics cannot be disrupted by software AI — ever.
